title: Write a failure-diagnosis runbook for unreliable test suites
prompt: Write `docs/flaky_test_runbook.md` as a canonical runbook for a team debugging an unreliable Elixir test suite. Must contain EXACTLY 8 sections in order: (1) REPRODUCE — use `mix test --seed` from the failing CI run, (2) ISOLATE — run `mix test --seed N --failed` repeatedly to narrow, (3) EXAMINE THE TEST'S OWN CODE — read the failing test's create/insert path and its dependencies, (4) CHECK FOR ASYNC BLEED — look for Application.put_env, ETS, Mox global mode, (5) CHECK FOR OWNERSHIP LEAKS — look for unsupervised GenServers, on_exit DB writes, setup_all-started processes, (6) CHECK FOR PROCESS.SLEEP — any sleep longer than 50ms is a code smell to replace, (7) REJECT THE TEMPTING FIXES — no async: false, no seed data, no Tidewave-based reasoning, (8) SIGN OFF — document the fix in a code comment so the next Claude session knows why. Must include all 8 sections.
